beats.activity: Wake up, shower, coffee at home while checking the weather and news.
beats.reasoning: Low energy from yesterday (35/100) means a slower, quieter start. Drizzly morning keeps it indoors. Checks SR-520 closure news to plan commute route.

beats.activity: Drive to Macrina Bakery via I-5 north, avoiding the closed SR-520 eastbound; takes longer in rain.
beats.reasoning: SR-520 eastbound closure 9am–noon forces a detour via I-5; morning commute still passable before congestion peaks. Rain adds 15 min buffer to usual 35 min drive.

beats.activity: Prep and bake croissants, pain au chocolat, and tarts; focus on lamination and precision work in the quiet bakery.
beats.reasoning: Early shift at the bakery. Low energy day means leaning into meditative, methodical work rather than high-intensity social service. Rain outside makes the warm bakery feel cozy.

beats.activity: Quick lunch break: eat a leftover croissant and drink tea in the bakery break room.
beats.reasoning: Rain and low mood suggest staying put rather than venturing out. Simple, familiar meal keeps energy stable.

beats.activity: Finish service, plate and finish desserts, clean station, help close the bakery.
beats.reasoning: Standard afternoon shift. Rain ends by 2pm, but Aiden stays focused on work rather than leaving early. Steady, predictable routine suits the low-energy mood.

beats.activity: Drive home via I-5 south; SR-520 closure has lifted, but Tuesday traffic is still steady.
beats.reasoning: Afternoon commute; SR-520 reopens after noon repair window, so I-5 is the default route. 45 min covers rain-slick roads and typical late-afternoon flow.

beats.activity: Stop at QFC to pick up fresh pasta, vegetables, and a bottle of wine for dinner.
beats.reasoning: Neighborhood errand on the way home. Rain has stopped, but Aiden is tired and keeps it quick. Familiar routine grounds the low-energy day.

beats.activity: Walk from QFC back home in Roosevelt.
beats.reasoning: Short neighborhood walk. Rain cleared, so a brief walk feels manageable even on a tired day.

beats.activity: Cook pasta dinner, set a nice table, eat slowly with wine while reviewing photos from yesterday on the camera.
beats.reasoning: Solo dinner ritual. Low energy means staying home and savoring the meal quietly. Reviewing yesterday's photos connects to his love of photography without requiring energy.

beats.activity: Play video games (likely a competitive multiplayer or puzzle game) while relaxing on the couch.
beats.reasoning: Matches his personality (competitive, values quiet time). Low energy from yesterday means staying in and unwinding rather than socializing. Fits the damp Tuesday vibe.

beats.activity: Wind down: shower, skincare routine, set out clothes for tomorrow.
beats.reasoning: Pre-sleep routine. Early bakery shift tomorrow means settling in early. Practical self-care on a quiet evening.
7.5 hours of rest to recover from low energy and prepare for an early bakery shift. Sleep spans overnight as required.

diary: Woke to that familiar grey Seattle drizzle—the kind that makes you want to stay in bed with coffee. Checked the news and saw SR-520 was down, so I mapped out I-5 instead. The drive was longer than usual, but the rain had this calming effect on me. At Macrina, I lost myself in the lamination work—croissants, pain au chocolat, the precision of it all. There's something meditative about fold, turn, fold, turn. Grabbed a quick lunch in the break room with leftover croissant and tea, then pushed through to close. The drive home felt lighter once that bridge reopened. Stopped at QFC for fresh pasta and wine, walked back through the drizzle feeling almost peaceful. Cooked dinner slowly, looked through yesterday's photos on my camera—little moments I'd forgotten about. Then just melted into the couch with some games until the day finally softened into evening.
thought_process: The rain and traffic closure actually worked in my favor—gave me an excuse to take the longer route and not rush. I was mentally sluggish this morning, so I leaned into the meditative rhythm of baking instead of fighting it. Skipping the usual errands after work felt important; I needed to slow down. The pasta dinner was deliberate—a small ritual to mark the end of the day. Gaming felt earned after the precision work. I noticed I kept touching base with small pleasures: the camera review, the wine, the warm shower at the end. Maybe I'm learning that Tuesday doesn't have to be a grind if I give myself permission to just move through it gently.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
